basics.name: Kota Kondo
basics.label: PhD Student
basics.summary: Graduate student at MIT AeroAstro working on multiagent systems, optimization, and learning-based trajectory planning for UAVs.

work.name: Astark Robotics
work.summary: Developing fully autonomous multiagent UAV systems for search and rescue operations in disaster recovery.

work.name: MIT Aerospace Controls Laboratory
work.summary: Multiagent trajectory planning, funded by Boeing Research & Technology. Advisor: Prof. Jonathan How.
highlights: Developed multiagent trajectory planner robust to communication delay

work.name: Stanford University, Space Rendezvous Laboratory
work.summary: The DWARF Satellite Project, Attitude Dynamics and Control System (ADCS). Advisor: Assoc. Prof. Simone D'Amico.
highlights: Built attitude and orbital simulator on C++/MATLAB Simulink

volunteer.summary: Organizing networking events and helping form a community in the New England region to strengthen the US-Japan relationship.

volunteer.organization: The Japan Society for Aeronautical and Space Sciences
volunteer.summary: Interviewed entrepreneurs, engineers, scientists, and professors in aerospace industry. Edited and published articles in JSASS journals.

awards.title: MathWorks Fellowship
awards.summary: Funded a full year of tuition, living stipend, and insurance costs.

awards.title: Recruit Scholarship
awards.summary: Funded three full years of tuition, living stipend, insurance costs, and travel for graduate studies.

awards.title: Funai Overseas Scholarship
awards.summary: Funded two full years of tuition, living stipend, insurance costs, and travel for graduate studies.

awards.title: James H.M. Sprayregen Scholarship
awards.summary: Financially supported to attend Aerospace conferences in Florida, Japan, and Australia.

awards.title: TOMODACHI Sumitomo Corporation Scholarship
awards.summary: Endowed $18,000 based on academic achievement to study at University of Michigan.

awards.title: ISTAT Foundation Scholarship
awards.awarder: International Society of Transport Aircraft Trading
awards.summary: Funded a one-year tuition senior year for academic excellence in aeronautics and aerospace engineering.

awards.title: Yamakawa Prize (President's Award)
awards.summary: Awarded for excellent academic record and acts for humanity, sociability, and internationality.
